name: ORBCOMM Industrial IoT & Fleet API
what_it_does: ORBCOMM Industrial IoT REST API for global asset tracking, fleet management, and cold chain monitoring using satellite and cellular connectivity. Enables AI agents to manage global container and asset GPS tracking via satellite and cellular networks for international logistics, handle refrigerated container and reefer temperature monitoring for cold chain compliance, access railroad car and heavy equipment tracking for industrial asset management, retrieve vessel and maritime tracking data for port and shipping operations, manage ELD and HOS compliance for commercial vehicle fleets, handle remote monitoring and control of industrial equipment via satellite, access real-time alarm and alert management for asset monitoring, retrieve fleet analytics and utilization reporting for operational intelligence, manage multi-network device management across satellite and cellular, and integrate ORBCOMM IoT data with TMS, ERP, and supply chain platforms.
use_cases: Tracking intermodal containers from global logistics agents
Monitoring reefer temperature from cold chain agents
Tracking railcars from rail logistics agents
Managing satellite-connected assets from remote monitoring agents
not_for: Urban-only fleet tracking without satellite coverage requirement
Simple passenger vehicle GPS without industrial IoT complexity
Small fleets without global satellite connectivity needs
best_when: A global logistics operator, intermodal carrier, railroad, or industrial equipment operator needing satellite IoT connectivity wants AI agents to automate global asset tracking, cold chain monitoring, and industrial equipment management.
avoid_when: COLD CHAIN RISK: Automated reefer temperature monitoring for food and pharmaceutical cargo creates FDA cold chain responsibility — automated temperature exceedance without human response protocol creates product liability and regulatory risk. Satellite IoT message latency varies (minutes to hours) for non-terrestrial coverage; automated workflows dependent on real-time satellite data must account for inherent latency. ORBCOMM was acquired by GreenLane Holdings in 2021 and rebranded; verify current API availability.

auth.notes: ORBCOMM uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. Developer portal at developer.orbcomm.com. Webhooks for IoT events. REST API with JSON and binary messaging. Reston, Virginia HQ. GreenLane Holdings acquisition (2021). ORBCOMM founded 1993 (originally satellite IoT). 3M+ devices connected globally. Strong in intermodal container, reefer, rail, and industrial IoT. Competes with Geotab, Samsara, and Trimble for commercial fleet telematics; unique in satellite IoT for maritime and global tracking.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-device monthly subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly per-device subscription; satellite airtime fees
pricing.notes: Reston, Virginia. ORBCOMM (GreenLane Holdings acquisition 2021). Founded 1993. 3M+ connected devices. Satellite and cellular IoT. Strong in container tracking, reefer, and rail. Competes with Geotab and Samsara for fleet; unique for satellite IoT coverage.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; message-based pricing for satellite

known_agent_gotchas: COLD CHAIN RISK: Reefer temperature exceedance automation creates FDA cold chain responsibility; automated product disposition without human authority creates food safety and product liability risk
Satellite message latency — satellite IoT messages have inherent latency (minutes to hours in non-cellular coverage); real-time automation workflows must account for satellite data delay
GreenLane acquisition transition — ORBCOMM acquisition by GreenLane 2021; verify current product branding, API availability, and company status
Satellite airtime cost management — satellite IoT messages have per-message costs; uncontrolled automated satellite message polling can generate significant airtime charges
Binary messaging protocol — ORBCOMM satellite communication uses binary messaging format for bandwidth efficiency; API parsing requires understanding of ORBCOMM binary message schema
Global coverage variability — ORBCOMM satellite coverage has polar coverage gaps and message delivery latency variations by orbital position; automation must handle message loss and retry
agent_readiness.security_notes: Industrial IoT/fleet. SOC2. OAuth2/API key. US/EU. Asset location and cargo sensor data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Industrial IoT. SOC2. OAuth2. US/EU. Asset and cargo data.
